title: Claude: Write, Research, Build

modules.title: What Claude Does Differently (and Why It Matters)
modules.sub: Understanding Claude's strengths before you start using them
modules.description: Learners explore what sets Claude apart from other AI tools and why those differences affect the kind of work you can do with it. You'll build a mental model of how Claude processes prompts so you can start making smarter requests from day one.

modules.title: Writing With Claude: Essays, Emails, and Side Projects
modules.sub: Using Claude as a real writing partner, not a shortcut
modules.description: You'll practice using Claude to draft, reshape, and sharpen actual writing across different formats — from persuasive essays to professional emails to passion projects. The focus is on staying in control of your voice while letting Claude do the heavy lifting on structure and iteration.

modules.title: Research Mode: Summarizing, Synthesizing, and Fact-Checking
modules.sub: Turning Claude into a research tool you can actually rely on
modules.description: Learners work through techniques for using Claude to summarize complex material, pull out key ideas, and pressure-test claims. You'll practice deciding what to trust, what to verify elsewhere, and how to use Claude's outputs as a starting point rather than a final answer.

modules.title: Building Something Real: Projects, Pitches, and Portfolios
modules.sub: Taking Claude from practice tool to project collaborator
modules.description: This module challenges you to use Claude to produce something tangible — a pitch deck outline, a portfolio piece, a project proposal — that you could actually show someone. You'll make decisions about how much to rely on Claude versus your own judgment and voice.

modules.title: Where Claude Falls Short and How to Work Around It
modules.sub: Knowing Claude's limits makes you a sharper user
modules.description: You'll examine the real failure modes — hallucinations, outdated knowledge, sycophantic responses — and learn concrete strategies for catching and correcting them. Leaving this module, you'll know exactly when to push back on Claude's output and how to prompt your way to something better.

synopsis: You've probably heard of Claude but maybe haven't figured out how to make it actually useful for your life â this course fixes that fast. You'll go from casual user to someone who uses Claude to produce real output: better writing, sharper research, and projects you can show people.
rationale: Claude is rapidly gaining traction among students and knowledge workers but lacks dedicated beginner-friendly guidance that connects it to real academic and side-project use cases.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can use Claude to draft, research, and refine work across writing-heavy tasks â and know exactly when to trust it and when to push back.
source_signals: Opus 4.5 is not the normal AI agent experience that I have had thus far
